title: Anchor All-Hands Q2 2026
compiled_truth:
The Q2 2026 all-hands for [Anchor Codec](companies/anchor) was held on July 1st, 2026 at the company's main office. All seven team members were present — a rarity given how often folks had been traveling for customer pilots in June. [Seren Voss](people/seren-voss) opened by walking through Q2 revenue numbers, which came in slightly above the internal stretch target. Two new design partners signed during the quarter, bringing the total to five. Seren was candid about the fact that one early pilot (with a logistics company) had stalled due to integration friction on the customer's side, not a technical issue per se but something the team needed to learn from.

[Dani Osei](people/dani-osei) gave the engineering update. The single-pass pipeline had hit its first major stability milestone — 72 hours of continuous operation under synthetic load without a crash or data divergence. Dani acknowledged that performance on ARM targets was lagging behind x86 by roughly 15%, and flagged this as a priority for Q3. [Miro Bergmann](people/miro-bergmann) followed up with a brief on the deterministic authentication layer, noting that edge-case handling around malformed headers had been tightened significantly. Miro also mentioned he'd been pair-programming with [Ari Leclerc](people/ari-leclerc) on the test harness, which had doubled coverage in the last six weeks.

[Tal Kimura](people/tal-kimura) presented the Q3 OKR draft. Key objectives included closing the first paid contract (not just design partnerships), shipping a public-facing benchmark suite, and hiring two more engineers. Tal pushed back on a suggestion from Seren to also target a conference talk in Q3, arguing the team was already stretched thin. There was a healthy ten-minute debate on this — ultimately the talk was moved to a Q4 stretch goal.

[Nova Mensah](people/nova-mensah) shared customer feedback themes. The most common request was better observability tooling — customers want to see what the pipeline is doing in real time, not just final outputs. [Paz Santos](people/paz-santos) volunteered to scope a lightweight dashboard prototype, estimating two weeks of work. Seren agreed to prioritize it if it could double as a demo asset for sales converations.

The meeting closed with Seren reminding everyone that the seed round runway extends through early 2028, but that hitting the Q3 revenue OKR would significantly strengthen the Series A narrative. Mood in the room was cautiously optimistic. Ari made coffee for everyone afterwards.
timeline: - **2026-06-15** | Final Q2 design partner signed after three-week evaluation; [Nova Mensah](people/nova-mensah) led the onboarding process.
- **2026-06-28** | [Dani Osei](people/dani-osei) confirmed 72-hour stability milestone on single-pass pipeline under synthetic load.
- **2026-07-01** | Q2 all-hands held with full team of seven; Q3 OKRs drafted and debated.
- **2026-07-14** | [Paz Santos](people/paz-santos) began scoping lightweight observability dashboard prototype.
- **2026-08-02** | [Miro Bergmann](people/miro-bergmann) and [Ari Leclerc](people/ari-leclerc) shipped expanded test harness with 2x coverage improvement.
- **2026-09-10** | [Tal Kimura](people/tal-kimura) circulated Q3 OKR scorecard internally ahead of quarterly review.
- **2026-10-01** | Q3 all-hands scheduled; first paid contract status to be reviewed.
- **2027-01-15** | [Seren Voss](people/seren-voss) targeted date for Series A preparation kickoff based on Q3/Q4 traction.

_facts.name: Anchor All-Hands Q2 2026
